name: Black-Eyed Pea Fritters
recipeInstructions:
1. When the peas have softened, rub off any remaining skins and soak them for an additional 30 minutes.
2. Drain and rinse the peas thoroughly.
3. In a food processor, process the peas, crushed garlic, salt, and black pepper. Add water gradually while continuing to process until you achieve a smooth, thick puree.
4. Preheat the oven to 250°F (120°C).
5. Heat oil in a large skillet to about 2-3 inches deep.
6. Using a spoon, drop tablespoons of batter into the hot oil and fry until golden brown. Repeat with the remaining batter.
7. Keep the fritters warm in the oven while you finish frying the rest.
8. Serve the fritters hot, sprinkled with salt and lime juice to taste.
(Note: This recipe is based on "Memories of a Cuban Kitchen" by Randelman & Schwartz.)
